description:
You are acting as a junior backup-engineer who has just pulled the nightly data
  into the staging area located at

        /home/user/backups/2023-10-03/

  The directory already contains exactly three regular files:

        db_dump.sql.gz
        photos.tar
        config.zip

  1. Produce a human-readable Markdown “inventory” document that lists the
     filename, its *exact* byte-size and its *exact* SHA-256 checksum.  
     Save the document to

        /home/user/backup_reports/2023-10-03-inventory.md

     a) File layout and style – the automated grader will check **every
        character**, so follow this template verbatim:

        ---
        date: 2023-10-03
        engineer: backup-bot
        ---

        # Backup Inventory Report – 2023-10-03

        | File | Size (bytes) | SHA256 |
        |------|--------------|--------|
        | config.zip | <bytes> | <hash> |
        | db_dump.sql.gz | <bytes> | <hash> |
        | photos.tar | <bytes> | <hash> |

        Total files: 3

        • The front-matter section uses three dashes (---) above **and** below.  
        • A single blank line must appear:
          – after the closing ---  
          – after the H1 heading  
          – after the table.  
        • Rows must be sorted alphabetically (case-sensitive) by filename.  
        • The table delimiter row must contain exactly six dashes under “File”,
          fourteen dashes under “Size (bytes)” and eight dashes under “SHA256”
          (see template).  
        • The document must end with a single newline (no extra blank lines).

  2. Lint the newly created Markdown file using the following three rules:

        R1  No line may exceed 120 characters.
        R2  No line may contain trailing white-space.
        R3  File must terminate with a newline.

     Produce a plain-text lint report at

        /home/user/backup_reports/2023-10-03-inventory.lint

     Required output format:

        Line-1:  “Lint results for 2023-10-03-inventory.md”
        If the file obeys all rules:
            Line-2:  “ALL CLEAR”
            (…and nothing else)
        If violations exist:
            One line per violation in the form
                <line_number>: <RULE_ID> <explanation>
            Do NOT include “ALL CLEAR” when violations are present.

     NOTE: The autograder will check for the literal string “ALL CLEAR”.  If it
     is missing, the grade is automatically a failure.

  3. Store everything inside “/home/user/backup_reports/”.  Create the directory
     if it does not yet exist and ensure it is readable by anyone and writable
     by the current user.

  4. Absolutely **do not** modify anything inside `/home/user/backups/`.

  5. On completion you should have:

        /home/user/backup_reports/2023-10-03-inventory.md
        /home/user/backup_reports/2023-10-03-inventory.lint

     with content matching the exact specifications above.  No other files
     should be created.

  Perform all work exclusively in the Linux terminal; no GUI tools.
truth:
<!-- Pre-populated backup data (already present when the task starts) -->
  <backup_dir>/home/user/backups/2023-10-03/</backup_dir>
  <files>
    <file>
      <name>db_dump.sql.gz</name>
      <content>SAMPLE DATABASE BACKUP 2023-10-03\n</content>
    </file>
    <file>
      <name>photos.tar</name>
      <content>PHOTOS ARCHIVE PLACEHOLDER\n</content>
    </file>
    <file>
      <name>config.zip</name>
      <content>CONFIG ZIP PLACEHOLDER\n</content>
    </file>
  </files>

  <!-- Expected artefacts to be produced by the agent -->
  <report>/home/user/backup_reports/2023-10-03-inventory.md</report>
  <lint>/home/user/backup_reports/2023-10-03-inventory.lint</lint>

  <!--
    The grading script will:

      • Verify that the Markdown file exists at the exact path above.
      • Compute the SHA-256 sums and byte-sizes of the three files inside the
        backup directory and compare them with the values that appear in the
        table of the Markdown document.
      • Check the front-matter, headings, table delimiters, spacing, row order
        and final “Total files: 3” line for exact matches.
      • Verify that the lint report exists, its first line is
        “Lint results for 2023-10-03-inventory.md” and its second line is
        “ALL CLEAR”.
      • Fail the test immediately if “ALL CLEAR” is not present or if additional
        lines exist below it.
  -->
